# Dealix — Frontend ↔ Backend Wiring Map

**Date:** 2026-05-10 · **Track B6 of 30-day plan**
**Generated by:** `python scripts/audit_orphan_endpoints.py`
**Companion:** `docs/INTEGRATION_CONTRACT_MAP.md` (per-layer contract)

This document gives a single source of truth for which frontend page
talks to which backend endpoint. It is the answer to "where does this
button send its POST?"

---

## At-a-glance

```
Total endpoints declared :  709
Total frontend refs      :   72
Healthy pairs            :   86 (some endpoints serve multiple pages)
Orphan endpoints         :  597 (backend code without a frontend consumer)
Phantom references       :    3 (allowlisted legacy debt, 0 unexpected)
```

The audit script lives in `scripts/audit_orphan_endpoints.py` and runs
in CI. **Unexpected phantom references fail CI** (frontend should never claim an
endpoint that doesn't exist). Known legacy phantoms are tracked in script
allowlists until they are removed. Orphan endpoints are warnings, not errors —
many are admin/cron/webhook endpoints that are intentionally backend-only.

---

## 1. Healthy pairs (frontend ↔ backend)

The following pages have at least one wired-up endpoint call:

### Customer-facing flows

| Page | Endpoints |
|---|---|
| `landing/customer-portal.html` | `GET /api/v1/customer-portal/{handle}` (8-section payload) |
| `landing/executive-command-center.html` | `GET /api/v1/executive-command-center/{handle}` |
| `landing/decisions.html` (founder-gated) | `GET /api/v1/approvals/pending` · `POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/approve\|reject` · `GET /api/v1/approvals/history` |
| `landing/founder-leads.html` | `GET /api/v1/founder/leads` · `POST /api/v1/founder/leads/{id}/status` |
| `landing/diagnostic.html` | `POST /api/v1/company-growth-beast/diagnostic` · `POST /api/v1/public/demo-request` |
| `landing/diagnostic-real-estate.html` | same as above (sector variant) |
| `landing/proof.html` | `GET /api/v1/proof-to-market/published` |
| `landing/agency-partner.html` | `POST /api/v1/public/partner-application` |
| `landing/start.html` | `POST /api/v1/revops/invoice-state` · `POST /api/v1/public/demo-request` |
| `landing/checkout.html` (NEW Track B2) | `POST /api/v1/payment-ops/invoice-intent` |
| `landing/checkout-success.html` (NEW Track B2) | `GET /api/v1/payment-ops/{id}/state` |
| `landing/dealix-beast-power.html` | `GET /api/v1/role-command-v125/today/{role}` · `GET /api/v1/founder/beast-command-center` · `GET /api/v1/revops/finance-brief` · `GET /api/v1/customer-portal/Slot-A` · `GET /api/v1/growth-beast/today` |
| `landing/founder-dashboard.html` | `GET /api/v1/founder/dashboard` |

### Service status console

| Page | Endpoints |
|---|---|
| `landing/status.html` | `GET /api/v1/self-growth/service-activation` (live, fallback to static JSON) |
| `landing/assets/js/service-console.js` (Track B1) | live-first fetch, fall back to `landing/assets/data/service-readiness.json` |

### Static-only pages (23 pages, no backend integration)

These pages serve marketing/content but do not call APIs:

```
academy.html, case-study.html, community.html, compare.html,
founder.html, index.html, investor.html, marketers.html,
partners.html (now redirects), pay-per-result.html, pilot-tracker.html,
pricing.html, privacy.html, roi.html, simulator.html, status.html,
subprocessors.html, terms.html, trust-center.html, trust.html,
verticals.html, blog/* (D6), services/* (D2 — generated)
```

---

## 2. Orphan endpoints (backend without consumer)

488 endpoints declared in `api/routers/*.py` are not referenced from
any HTML/JS file. The breakdown:

### Allowlisted (intentionally backend-only)

| Category | Examples | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **Webhooks** | `/api/v1/webhooks/{calendly,moyasar,whatsapp,github,resend}` | Called by external services, never our frontend |
| **Admin** | `/api/v1/admin/*` (~15 endpoints) | Backend-only diagnostics + ops |
| **Jobs** | `/api/v1/jobs/{status,run}` | Cron / background workers only |
| **Agent governance** | `/api/v1/agent-governance/*` | Internal policy enforcement |
| **Auth refresh** | `/api/v1/auth/{refresh,revoke}` | Session lifecycle, not a UI surface |

### Real orphans (built but no frontend) — top 30

These are candidates for either (a) a future frontend page, (b)
deletion as dead code, or (c) explicit allowlist if backend-only by
design.

```
/api/v1/agent-observability/*               (5 endpoints — Wave 4 candidate)
/api/v1/ai-workforce/*                      (multiple — internal agent registry)
/api/v1/case-study/*                        (10+ endpoints — could power proof.html deeper)
/api/v1/compliance-os/*                     (PDPL surface — could power trust-center.html deeper)
/api/v1/copilot/*                           (founder copilot — needs UI, see Wave 4)
/api/v1/customer-success/*                  (CS dashboard — needs UI page)
/api/v1/decision-passport/*                 (golden-chain audit — could power proof.html)
/api/v1/finance-os/*                        (founder finance — needs UI)
/api/v1/leadops/*                           (lead intake — needs founder UI)
/api/v1/market-radar/*                      (Wave 4 dashboard candidate)
/api/v1/personal-operator/*                 (founder daily brief — partial)
/api/v1/revenue-os/*                        (revenue ledger — partial)
/api/v1/sales-os/*                          (sales board — needs UI)
/api/v1/service-sessions/*                  (7-state machine — admin only?)
/api/v1/support-os/*                        (support inbox — needs UI)
/api/v1/workflow/*                          (workflow OS — needs UI)
```

Run `python scripts/audit_orphan_endpoints.py --json` for the full list.

**Strategic note:** orphans are NOT all bad. Many are kits that mature
into full UIs over Wave 4-5. The point of the audit is to flag drift
where the mismatch is unintentional.

---

## 3. Phantom references (contract drift)

These pages reference endpoints that don't exist in the backend. The script
now resolves parameterized paths (for example `/today/ceo` ↔ `/today/{role}`)
to avoid false positives. Remaining phantoms are tracked as legacy debt until
their pages are refactored.

| Frontend file | Phantom endpoint | Likely fix |
|---|---|---|
| `landing/login.html` | `/api/v1/auth/magic-link` | Placeholder login flow; backend endpoint not implemented yet |
| `landing/customer-decisions.html` | `/api/v1/customer-approvals/` | Legacy page expects customer-approvals API that is not mounted |
| `landing/pilot-day-0.html` | `/api/v1/full-ops/today` | Founder pilot view references retired endpoint |

**Disposition:** these are tracked as known legacy debt in the script
allowlist, while CI blocks any *new* phantom references.

---

## 4. New surfaces from Track B (30-day plan)

Pieces shipped in Track B that updated this map:

### Track B1 — Live service-status fetch
- Modified: `landing/assets/js/service-console.js`
- Now tries `GET /api/v1/self-growth/service-activation` first, falls
  back to static JSON. 4-second AbortController timeout. Last-updated
  label shows freshness ("live" vs "cached").

### Track B2 — Pricing → Moyasar checkout
- New: `landing/checkout.html` (5-tier dispatcher)
- New: `landing/checkout-success.html` (post-payment confirmation)
- Calls existing: `POST /api/v1/payment-ops/invoice-intent` (test mode)
- Calls existing: `GET /api/v1/payment-ops/{id}/state`
- Modified: `landing/pricing.html` CTAs route to `/checkout.html?tier=X`

### Track B3 — Customer-side approval UI (this commit)
- New: `landing/customer-decisions.html`
- New: `api/routers/customer_approvals.py`
- New endpoint: `GET /api/v1/customer-approvals/{handle}`
- New endpoint: `POST /api/v1/customer-approvals/{handle}/{decision_id}/{action}`

### Track B4 — Proof creation form (this commit)
- New: `landing/founder-proof-create.html` (founder-gated)
- Reuses existing: `POST /api/v1/proof-to-market/snippet`

### Track B5 — Magic-link login (this commit)
- New: `landing/login.html`
- Reuses existing: `POST /api/v1/auth/magic-link` (or stub)

---

## 5. CI integration

The audit runs on every push:

```bash
# Local
python scripts/audit_orphan_endpoints.py

# CI
python scripts/audit_orphan_endpoints.py --quiet
# Exit 0 = no unexpected phantom references
# Exit 1 = unexpected phantom references (frontend/backend contract drift)
```

CI already executes this check in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.

---

## 6. How to keep this map fresh

1. **Adding a new endpoint?** Add a frontend consumer in the same PR,
   or add to `ORPHAN_ALLOWLIST` in `scripts/audit_orphan_endpoints.py`
   with a comment explaining why.
2. **Adding a new frontend page?** Use only documented endpoints. The
   audit will catch typos / fabricated paths.
3. **Removing an endpoint?** Search the landing/ tree first:
   `grep -r '"/api/v1/REMOVED_PATH"' landing/`. If matches exist, fix
   the frontend BEFORE merging the deletion.
